Title: AtMYB4: a transcription factor general in the battle against UV

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represses sinapoylmalate accumulation by repressing C4H expression; loss of AtMYB4 increases sinapoylmalate and UV resistance
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtMYB4 was identified as an Arabidopsis R2R3-MYB that negatively regulates sinapate ester biosynthesis. 2. Disruption of AtMYB4 increased C4H mRNA levels and elevated sinapoylmalate accumulation in leaves. 3. AtMYB4 expression was down-regulated by UV-B, and the atmyb4 mutant showed increased UV resistance.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: general phenylpropanoid pathway; sinapate ester biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key negative regulator of UV-protective sinapate ester accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: insertional mutant analysis; quantitative RT-PCR of phenylpropanoid genes; metabolite analysis of sinapoylmalate; UV-B response phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: down-regulated in wild-type plants exposed to UV-B
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This article is a Research News summary, not a primary research article. It describes AtMYB4 as the first MYB protein shown to function as a transcriptional repressor at the physiological level.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to enhance UV-protective sinapate ester accumulation and UV tolerance by reducing AtMYB4 activity.
